rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] oxidative annulation of 4-methoxystyrene with N-Boc-4-hydroxyaniline to form a 2,3-dihydrobenzofuran via radical intermediates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the para-hydroxyaniline (and/or styrene) to generate phenoxyl/aryl radical and quinone-imine type intermediates that couple with the styrene

key_intermediates.name: phenoxyl/aminyl radical from N-Boc-4-hydroxyaniline (species II, oxidized aryloxy radical with N-Boc)
benzylic C-radical adduct from styrene addition to phenoxyl radical (species III)
ortho-quinone-imine/methide-like electrophile leading to cyclization to dihydrobenzofuran 3a

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes N-Boc-4-hydroxyaniline 2a to a phenoxyl/N-centered radical (II); this radical adds to the terminal carbon of 4-methoxystyrene 1a, generating a stabilized benzylic radical (III); further oxidation and intramolecular O-attack closes the 2,3-dihydrobenzofuran ring of 3a, with H2 evolution at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (only trace 3); BHT adducts 4a (with aryloxy/aminyl radical, HRMS [M+Na]+ 450.2617) and 4b (with benzylic radical from styrene, HRMS [M+Na]+ 584.3335) detected

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (only trace 3); TEMPO adducts 5a (HRMS [M+Na]+ 387.2271) and 5b (HRMS [M+H]+ 499.3166) detected, supporting both aryloxy radical and benzylic radical intermediates

notes: Current screening (Table S6) identified 10 mA as optimal (93% yield, 2 h). Mechanism proposal is supported only by radical trapping with HRMS detection of adducts; no KIE, CV, or DFT data shown on these pages.
